D8 AnthropologyL2 UnderstandReference

Saffron Robes — Buddhist Sangha Across Asia

Buddhist robes (kāṣāya) take saffron-yellow in Theravada, deep maroon in Tibetan Vajrayana, grey-charcoal in Japanese Zen — same vow, different dye-stuff economy

The Buddha specified rag-robes dyed with cheap, locally available natural materials — turmeric/saffron in South Asia (yellow), madder + bark in Tibet (maroon), gall + iron in Japan (grey). Same monastic vow, locally adapted hue. Modern saffron robes are mostly cotton dyed with synthetic azo dyes matching the traditional shade.

Pick tradition.
